methods.description: A two-dimensional approach to analyze enamel thickness and enamel-dentine junction (EDJ) morphology due to diagenesis and wear of molars, using virtual 2D section planes generated from 3D models.

steps.description: Virtual 2D section planes of the mesial cusps were generated from 3D models with VG Studio MAX 2.0 software.
steps.searchable_summary: generate 2D section planes from 3D models using VG Studio MAX 2.0

steps.description: Several variables were quantified on 2D section planes with Sigma Scan Pro software interfaced to a Wacom digitizing tablet.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify variables on 2D section planes using Sigma Scan Pro

steps.description: Average enamel thickness (AET) was calculated as [c/e], yielding the average straight-line distance from the enamel-dentine junction to the outer enamel surface.
AET was scaled for comparisons between taxa of different size by calculation of relative enamel thickness (RET).
Molar EDJ morphology was quantified by collecting nine landmarks and semi-landmarks from each intact and lightly worn cross-section.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify molar EDJ morphology using landmarks

steps.description: The relative distances were subjected to discriminant function analysis (DFA) using SPSS software.
steps.searchable_summary: perform discriminant function analysis (DFA) using SPSS

methods.description: Analysis of dental development using phase contrast SR microCT to assess long-period line periodicity and crown formation times from virtual histological slices and 3D models.

steps.description: Phase contrast SR microCT was employed to assess long-period line periodicity and crown formation times from virtual histological slices and 3D models.
steps.searchable_summary: use phase contrast SR microCT for virtual histological analysis

steps.description: The periodicity, or number of daily lines between successive long-period incremental features, was examined in 23 fossil teeth.
The Mann-Whitney U test was performed for comparisons of periodicity values between H. erectus and fossil Pongo teeth.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Mann-Whitney U test for periodicity comparison

steps.description: Crown formation time was calculated as the sum of the cuspal and lateral enamel formation times.
Cuspal formation time was estimated by dividing the linear enamel thickness of each tooth cusp by 4.2 μm/day.
Lateral enamel formation time was calculated by multiplying the number of enamel long-period lines by the long-period line periodicity.
